steps.title: Convert the percentage to a decimal
Classify the function
steps.content:
$f(x) = 2.93x$ has the form $f(x) = mx + b$ with $m = 2.93$ and $b = 0$ — this is a linear function (not exponential, which would have $x$ in an exponent).

The slope $m = 2.93 > 0$, so as $x$ increases, $f(x)$ increases. The function is increasing.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: Decreasing exponential — incorrect on both counts; the function is linear, not exponential, and it increases
  • B: Decreasing linear — correctly identifies linear, but the positive slope makes it increasing, not decreasing
  • C: Increasing exponential — correctly identifies increasing, but the function has no exponent on $x$, so it is linear, not exponential
  • D: Increasing linear — matches both the form ($mx+b$) and the positive slope
That matches choice D — D is correct.
